## Query: ""BeatLab" music app rhythm game beat maker trademark"

I found at least one relevant U.S. trademark for **“Beat Lab”**, but the web results do **not** clearly show a registered mark specifically for **“BeatLab” as a music app / rhythm game / beat-maker software**.

What I found:
- A live U.S. registration for **“Beat Lab”** exists under **Reg. No. 5,599,403 / Serial No. 87800205**, owned by **Beat Lab** in Los Angeles, filed **February 16, 2018** and registered **November 6, 2018**. The result shown is a **design mark** rather than a plain-word mark. Source: [USPTO.report listing](https://uspto.report/TM/87800205/) and USPTO resources on [trademark search](https://www.uspto.gov/trademarks/search/search), [TSDR](https://tsdr.uspto.gov/).  
- There is also a related filing for **“BEATLAB ACADEMY”** tied to the same 2018 serial-number block, suggesting broader Beat Lab branding activity. Source: [Justia trademark page](https://trademarks.justia.com/878/00/beat-lab-87800207.html).
- Separately, “BeatLab” appears in app/store usage for rhythm or music-related apps, including an iOS app described as a rhythm/timing tool and a dance/musician practice app, but store presence alone does **not** prove trademark rights or registration: [Apple App Store](https://apps.apple.com/us/app/beatlab-metronome-beats/id6755929889), [BeatLab app site](https://beatlab.app/en/).

Bottom line:
- **Yes, “Beat Lab” is already present in U.S. trademark records.**
- Based on these web results, **“BeatLab” for a music app, rhythm game, or beat maker looks potentially risky from a clearance perspective**, especially because the names are very close and the goods/services are music-adjacent.
- But I **cannot conclude infringement or registrability** from these search snippets alone; that requires a proper USPTO search by classes/goods and likely a legal review.

Best next step:
- Run a full USPTO clearance search via [Trademark Search](https://www.uspto.gov/trademarks/search/search) and inspect the record in [TSDR](https://tsdr.uspto.gov/), focusing on software classes and wording like **downloadable software, music production software, game software, mobile apps**.

If you want, I can do a **deeper clearance-style search** for “BeatLab” across USPTO, app stores, and common-law use and summarize the risk.

## Query: ""SongLab" music app software trademark"

A quick web check suggests **“SONGLAB” is already the subject of multiple U.S. trademark filings/records**, including ones tied to music-related software/services, so it is **not a clean name for a music app/software brand**.

Key hits:
- **SONGLAB — SourceAudio Holdings, LLC**: application covers **SaaS software using AI for generating, sorting, transcribing, and mixing music** for the music/media industries. That is very close to “music app software.” [Justia record](https://trademarks.justia.com/988/04/songlab-98804771.html)
- **SONGLAB — SongLab**: older filing tied to **songwriting/music-writing-related services**. [Furm record](https://furm.com/trademarks/songlab-86613142)
- **SONG LAB — Song Lab, Inc.** also appears in trademark records as a similar mark. [USPTO.report record](https://uspto.report/TM/98598106)
- USPTO’s official search tools for verifying status/details are here: [Trademark Search](https://www.uspto.gov/trademarks/search), [tmsearch](https://tmsearch.uspto.gov/?page=help), and [TSDR status lookup](https://tsdr.uspto.gov/)

Bottom line: **Using “SongLab” for a music app/software product likely carries meaningful trademark conflict risk**, especially because at least one identified filing is in highly similar software/music categories.

# BeatForge — REX Player, Drum Machine & Step Sequencer Plugin

## Core Capabilities

Hardware-feel workflow with the depth of a modern sound design environment.

### 30+ Drum Synth Models

Full **808 and 909 kits** circuit-modeled — kicks, snares, hats, toms, claps, cymbals, congas, claves. Plus **Vintage DCO, Hoover, Reese Bass, Supersaw, FM Kick, and Karplus Snare**. All synthesized, no samples.

### Authentic Tube Saturation

Real **Koren 12AX7 valve modeling** with asymmetric saturation and natural second-harmonic warmth. Plus Soft, Hard, Foldback, and Hardcore modes for everything from analog gloss to total destruction.

### 32 Edit Groups Per Track

Up to **32 distinct sound variations per track**, assignable per step. One kick track, dozens of kicks across the pattern. Build entire fills, ghost notes, accent layers, and humanized variations on a single track. No other drum machine plugin lets you go this deep without leaving the row.

### 10 LFOs + Shape Editor

Draw **custom LFO curves** with tension control. Sync to host, free-run, or trigger per step. Per-target bypass without losing depth. **5-stage AHDSR** envelopes with adjustable tension on every stage.

### Per-Step Parameter Locks

Every knob is automatable per-step. **Pitch, filter, distortion, synth shape, bit depth, sample rate** — anything. Shift-select steps, move a knob, done. Hit chance, sub-steps, and polyrhythm endpoints built in.

New

### Generative Fills (GEN)

**Euclidean fill** with a live HITS knob — hear the pattern morph as you turn. **Dice & density** seeded random fills, plus rotation arrows to shift any pattern left or right. Happy accidents, on demand.

New

### 808-Style Accents

A dedicated **per-step accent row** with a global accent amount — the classic hardware trick for grooves that breathe. Accents carry through to **MIDI export** so your DAW clip keeps the dynamics.

New

### Per-Track Speed

Run any track at **1/4× to 4× the pattern speed**, triplet ratios included. Half-time kicks under double-time hats, polytempo textures, instant drum & bass — all inside one pattern.

New

### Link Groups

Tag **steps or REX slices into colored groups** and edit them as one. Shift-click recalls a group's settings everywhere it appears. Build variations that stay consistent across the whole pattern.

New

### Multi-Out Slice Routing

Route **individual REX slices or whole link groups to separate plugin outputs**. Process the snare slices through your favorite bus chain while the rest stays on the main out. Remixing, solved.

### Vintage Lo-Fi Topology

Hardware-accurate sampler emulation. **Anti-aliasing, zero-order hold, noise-shaped bitcrushing**, and 3 character shapes: Arcade, Vintage Sampler, and Crunch. Per-track lo-fi without compromising the rest of the mix.

### BeatForge Sculptor EQ

Dedicated drum-tuned EQ with five musical bands: **Sub, Thump, Clean, Crack, and Air**. Each band targets specific drum frequencies — boost punch at 120Hz, snap at 3kHz, presence at 10kHz. Tube exciter on the high band.

### Full MIDI Control

MIDI Learn on every knob, every step, every button. Native **Launch Control XL** integration with bidirectional LED feedback. Full MIDI I/O per track. Drag-and-drop MIDI export of any pattern or REX slice.

### Smart Sample Engine

**AI-powered BPM detection** via Beat This!. Native REX support plus a built-in slicer for WAV/AIFF/FLAC/MP3/OGG. **Random sample & slice modes**, time-stretch or resample pitch, full undo/redo. Drag-and-drop everything.

## BeatForge In Action

More demos and walkthroughs on our [YouTube channel](https://www.youtube.com/@beatforgesequencer).

## User Manual

Master the workflow: from AI-assisted importing to per-step parameter locking.

BeatForge runs as a Standalone App and as a VST3/AU Plugin. The installer handles placement automatically.

#### Installation

*   **Windows:** Run the `.msi` installer. You can select which components to install (Standalone/VST3). The installer will automatically place plugins in the correct system folder (`C:\Program Files\Common Files\VST3`) so your DAW can find them.
*   **macOS:** Open the `.dmg` file. Drag the BeatForge app to your **Applications** folder. To install the plugins (VST3/AU), run the separate `.pkg` installer found inside the disk image.

#### Activation

*   **Free Trial:** On first launch, click "Start Trial" to begin a fully functional 14-day evaluation.
*   **Purchase:** Click the "Buy Now" button on our website. You will receive a license code via email from **Lemon Squeezy** immediately after purchase.
*   **Unlock:** Copy the code from your email and paste it into the BeatForge activation screen to unlock your lifetime license.

Navigate using the large buttons at the top of the interface:

*   **Sequencer:** The main hub. Manage tracks, sequencing, LFOs, and sound design (Mix, ADSR, FX, EQ, Filter, Synth).
*   **Mixer:** A dedicated view for quick **audio and MIDI routing**. Use this to assign Audio Outputs and set MIDI Input/Output channels for all tracks side-by-side. The Volume and Pan sliders here are **Master Controls** that sit _after_ the sequencer's internal mixing chain.
*   **Slicer:** The advanced sample editor. View waveforms, detect transients, and slice standard audio loops. _(Note: REX files are **read-only** in this view)._

BeatForge integrates the advanced [Beat This!](https://github.com/CPJKU/beat_this) AI algorithm to analyze samples instantly upon import.

*   **Drag & Drop:** Drop any WAV, AIFF, FLAC, MP3, OGG, or REX (.rx2) file onto a track slot.
*   **Auto-Sync:** The engine detects the sample's BPM automatically.
    *   If you play the loop **unsliced**, BeatForge automatically applies **Time Stretching** to lock it to your project tempo.
    *   If you **Slice** the loop, synchronization is handled by the sequencer triggering individual slices at the correct time, keeping your transients crisp without stretching artifacts.
*   **Manual Correction:** In the rare case the detected BPM is incorrect, you can click to enter it manually. You can also hold **Shift** and drag the grid lines near the end of the loop to fine-tune the BPM for a perfect match.

Create evolving patterns using Edit Groups, P-Locks, and probability.

#### Basic Sequencing

*   **Step Buttons:** Click to toggle steps. Right-Click a step to set **Hit Chance** (Probability), **Flam/Rolls**, or assign an Edit Group.
*   **Grid Size:** Right-click the **Track Name** to toggle the track between **16 Steps** (Standard) and **12 Steps** (Triplets/8T).
*   **Polymeters:** Alt + Click a step to set it as the "Bar Endpoint." That track will loop independently at that step count.
*   **Track Speed:** Set any track to run from **1/4× to 4×** the pattern speed, including triplet ratios — half-time and double-time feels inside one pattern.
*   **Accents:** Toggle the accent row to push individual steps harder, 808-style. The global **Accent** slider sets how much louder accented steps hit, and accents are preserved in MIDI export.

#### Edit Groups (32 per track)

Each track has **32 Edit Groups** — 32 fully independent sound configurations that can be assigned per step. Every knob position, synth type, distortion mode, EQ setting, and macro is stored separately per group. Use them for true per-step sound design within a single track:

*   **Switch Active Group:** Use the Group selector at the top of the Sequencer to choose which group your knob edits affect. The synth tab, FX, EQ — everything reflects the currently selected group.
*   **Assign Steps to Groups:** Scroll on a step button to assign it to a different group. The step shows a small group number when it's not Group 1.
*   **Use Cases:**
    *   Tight short kick on the downbeats, longer distorted kick on the off-beats — all on one track.
    *   Build a whole drum fill on one track by varying the synth type per group.
    *   Create ghost notes with different velocity, decay, and filter — without touching another track.
    *   Layer accent variations: open hat on beat 2 and 4, closed hat everywhere else, same track.

#### Parameter Locks (P-Locks)

Automate any knob per-step without complex menus:

*   **Select Step(s):** Hold **Shift** and click a step button (or multiple) to select them. They will remain highlighted.
*   **Lock Parameter:** With the step(s) selected, move _any_ slider (Pitch, Filter, Decay, etc.). The slider will indicate a lock is set for those steps.
*   **Clear Lock:** Right-Click any step to turn off its locks. To clear multiple steps at once, **Shift-Select** them first, then **Right-Click**.

The **GEN tab** writes patterns for you — as a starting point or a finished groove.

*   **Euclidean Fill:** Turn the **HITS** knob to spread that many hits as evenly as possible across the track. You hear the pattern update live while turning; release to commit, or leave the knob where it started to keep your original pattern.
*   **Dice & Density:** **DICE** rolls a new random pattern, **DENS** sets how busy it gets. The same dice value always produces the same pattern at a given density, so you can dial density up and down without losing a roll you like.
*   **Rotate:** The arrow buttons shift the whole pattern left or right by one step. Modifier keys change what gets rotated — try them with Shift and Alt held.

Everything the GEN tab writes is a normal pattern afterwards — edit steps, add parameter locks, assign edit groups as usual. And if you don't like a result: full **undo/redo** has your back.

BeatForge ships with 30+ circuit-modeled drum synths. Switch any track to "Synth" mode and pick from the dropdown.

#### 808 Kit (Eight-O)

Full 808 model: Kick, Snare, Closed Hat, Open Hat, Cymbal, Cowbell, Clap, Low/Mid/Hi Tom, Low/Mid/Hi Conga, Rimshot, Claves, Maracas.

#### 909 Kit (Nine-O)

Full 909 model: Kick, Snare, Rimshot, Clap, Low/Mid/Hi Tom, Closed Hat, Open Hat, Crash, Ride.

#### Bass & Lead Synths

*   **Vintage DCO:** Classic analog-style oscillator. Warm, characterful, great for chiptune and 80s tones.
*   **Hoover:** The iconic gabber/hardcore lead. Detuned, raw, ready to mangle.
*   **Reese Bass:** Thick, detuned bass with wobble character. Drum & bass essential.
*   **Supersaw:** Seven detuned saws stacked. Trance/EDM/euphoric leads.
*   **FM Kick:** Frequency modulation kick for punchy, modern electronic kicks beyond what 808/909 can do.
*   **Karplus Snare:** Physical modeling with coupled drum skins (A/B interaction) and snare wires for realistic acoustic response.

#### Macro Knobs

Every synth has 4 macro controls: **Shape, Timbre, Color, Aux**. The exact parameters they control vary by synth, but the macros are always musically useful — twist them to discover the engine's range.

BeatForge features two distinct slicing engines depending on your file type.

#### 1\. REX Files (.rx2)

REX files are loaded **Read-Only**. There is no need to open the Slicer tab for these, as the slice markers are pre-defined (use Reason Studios' ReCycle to edit them). Instead, focus on the **Playback Mode (S/L/C)** toggle next to the track name:

*   **Slice (S):** Play individual slices using MIDI notes. (_Tip: Ensure your track's **MIDI Input Channel** is set correctly in the **Mixer Tab** to receive notes from your DAW_).
*   **Loop (L):** The loop plays continuously, locked to the host tempo.
*   **Sequence (C):** "Creative" mode. Allows you to select a specific start slice/sequence and trigger it using BeatForge's internal step buttons.

**MIDI Export:** You can drag the REX MIDI pattern directly from the track to your DAW timeline.

#### Working With Slices

*   **Mute a Slice:** Option/Alt + Click a slice to mute it in place — it stays muted until you unmute it or load a new file. Great for thinning out busy loops.
*   **Slice Parameter Locks:** Select a slice and move any knob to lock that setting to the slice. Slice locks apply _both_ in the internal sequencer and when triggering via MIDI from your DAW.
*   **Link Groups:** Right-click a slice to tag it into a colored group. Shift + Click a group chip to recall its settings on every slice in the group.
*   **Per-Slice Outputs:** Right-click a slice (or a link group) to override its audio output. Anything without an override falls back to the track's Main output.
*   **Random Slice Mode:** Enable Random on the track and every trigger picks a fresh slice — muted and empty slices are skipped automatically.

#### 2\. Audio Slicer (WAV/AIFF/FLAC)

For standard audio, open the **Slicer Tab** to access the full editor.

*   **Editing:** Use the **Sensitivity** slider to auto-detect transients, or manually Move, Delete, and **Lock** slice markers directly on the waveform.
*   **MIDI Export:** Once you've sliced the loop, drag the MIDI pattern directly from the track to your DAW timeline to generate a matching MIDI clip — one note per slice.

High-quality time-stretching and pitch-shifting are powered by [Signalsmith Stretch](https://signalsmith-audio.co.uk/code/stretch/) by Geraint Luff / Signalsmith Audio, used under the MIT License.

#### Distortion (5 modes)

Right-click the Distortion knob to select one of 5 algorithms:

*   **Soft:** Warm saturation with rounded peaks. Default character.
*   **Hard:** Aggressive digital clipping.
*   **Foldback:** Wave-folding distortion that adds metallic, resonant harmonics.
*   **Hardcore:** Extreme high-gain drive (10x boost) with a soft-knee limiter.
*   **Tube:** Authentic **Koren 12AX7 valve modeling**. Asymmetric saturation with real second-harmonic content. The most musical of the five — use for analog warmth and natural character.

#### Vintage Lo-Fi (Bitcrusher + Decimator)

Two sliders give you authentic hardware-sampler character:

*   **Bit Depth:** Reduce from 16-bit down to 2-bit. Right-click to select shape:
    *   _Arcade:_ Clean digital quantization.
    *   _Vintage Sampler:_ Aggressive noise-shaped quantization with vintage character.
    *   _Crunch:_ Hybrid — between clean and vintage.
*   **Sample Rate:** Hardware-accurate downsampling with proper anti-aliasing, zero-order hold, and DAC-style reconstruction filtering. Get authentic SP-1200 or MPC60 character without digital nastiness.

#### BeatForge Sculptor EQ

Five drum-tuned bands targeting specific musical frequencies:

*   **Sub (60Hz boost + 100Hz cut):** Classic Pultec sub trick. Adds weight without mud.
*   **Thump (120Hz):** Chest-hitting punch.
*   **Clean (400Hz):** Boxy lower-mid control.
*   **Crack (3kHz):** Aggressive transient snap.
*   **Air (10kHz):** High shelf routed through asymmetric tube exciter for harmonic sizzle.

**High Pass / Low Pass:** Standalone resonant filters for additional tone shaping. Fully automatable.

#### Advanced Envelope (AHDSR)

Precision transient shaping with a 5-stage envelope:

*   **Stages:** Attack, **Hold**, Decay, Sustain, Release. The "Hold" stage keeps the volume at max before decay, essential for punchy body.
*   **Tension Curves:** Adjust the slope of the Attack, Decay, and Release stages independently. Morph from **Logarithmic** (snappy) to **Exponential** (lazy).

BeatForge gives you **10 LFOs**, each with a fully editable shape and a deep routing system.

#### LFO Shape Editor

*   **Draw Your Own:** Click to add nodes, drag to position them. Mid-segment handles control curve tension (logarithmic to exponential).
*   **Presets:** Sine, Square, Saw Up, Saw Down — all built from the same node system, so you can modify them after loading.
*   **Sync Modes:** Tempo-synced (1/16th, 1/8, 1/4, etc. with triplet/dotted options) or free-running in Hz.
*   **Trigger Modes:** Global (always running), Trigger (restart on each note), Envelope (one-shot on trigger).

#### Routing

*   **Drag & Drop:** Drag any LFO onto any knob to assign modulation.
*   **Bipolar/Unipolar:** Right-click to toggle modulation polarity.
*   **Bypass:** Right-click "Bypass LFO" to temporarily disable without losing your depth setting. Perfect for live A/B comparison.
*   **Live Visualization:** The modulated knob shows the LFO's current value as a moving dot on the ring.

BeatForge has deep MIDI integration designed for hardware-first workflows.

#### MIDI Learn

*   **Universal Assignment:** Right-click any knob, button, or step to assign a MIDI CC or Note.
*   **Mute/Solo Buttons:** Assign to hardware buttons for tactile mixing.
*   **Step Buttons:** Assign to pads or step sequencer hardware for direct programming.

#### Launch Control XL Integration

Native support for Novation Launch Control XL with bidirectional LED feedback:

*   **Sequencer Flash:** Hardware buttons light up in sync with the playing step on the selected track.
*   **Mute/Solo Sync:** Hardware LEDs reflect current mute/solo state.
*   **Track Selection:** Switch tracks via MIDI and the hardware automatically follows.

#### MIDI I/O

*   **Per-Track Input:** Each track listens on its own MIDI channel for triggering.
*   **MIDI Export:** Drag any pattern or REX slice arrangement directly from BeatForge to your DAW.

## Download & Purchase

Start with the free trial. Buy when you're ready — your license is perpetual.

### macOS

(VST3 / AU / Standalone)  
macOS 11+ · Apple Silicon & Intel

[Download DMG](https://github.com/Paulllux/BeatForge/releases/latest/download/BeatForge.dmg)

**System requirements:** macOS 11 (Big Sur) or later (Apple Silicon & Intel, universal binary) · Windows 10 or later, 64-bit · Runs in any 64-bit VST3/AU host or standalone.

Already convinced? Unlock your perpetual license.

## REX Files — Frequently Asked Questions

### How do I play REX files in Ableton Live?

Ableton Live dropped native REX support in Live 11. Load BeatForge as a VST3 instrument, drag your .rx2 file in, and the slices map to the step sequencer with tempo-locked playback — no conversion, no time-stretching.

### Can Logic Pro, Cubase or FL Studio open .rx2 files?

Not natively in current versions. BeatForge runs as a VST3/AU plugin in all of them and reads .rx2 files directly through the official Reason Studios REX SDK.

### Is BeatForge like Dr. Octo Rex outside Reason?

Same idea, different host: BeatForge brings Octo Rex-style REX loop playback — slice triggering, sequenced slices, tempo sync — to any VST3/AU DAW, and adds a full drum machine, per-step parameter locks and multi-output slice routing around it.

### How do I create new REX files in 2026?

With ReCycle itself — Reason Studios re-released it in 2025 as a free download, with Apple Silicon and Windows ARM support. Slice your loops in ReCycle, then play them in any DAW with BeatForge: a complete REX workflow where the creation tool costs nothing. BeatForge's own slicer also chops WAV, AIFF, FLAC, MP3 and OGG directly.

More questions? Email [**beatforgehelp@gmail.com**](mailto:beatforgehelp@gmail.com) — you'll get the developer, not a ticket queue.

# BeatForge — REX Player, Drum Machine & Step Sequencer Plugin

## Core Capabilities

Hardware-feel workflow with the depth of a modern sound design environment.

### 30+ Drum Synth Models

Full **808 and 909 kits** circuit-modeled — kicks, snares, hats, toms, claps, cymbals, congas, claves. Plus **Vintage DCO, Hoover, Reese Bass, Supersaw, FM Kick, and Karplus Snare**. All synthesized, no samples.

### Authentic Tube Saturation

Real **Koren 12AX7 valve modeling** with asymmetric saturation and natural second-harmonic warmth. Plus Soft, Hard, Foldback, and Hardcore modes for everything from analog gloss to total destruction.

### 32 Edit Groups Per Track

Up to **32 distinct sound variations per track**, assignable per step. One kick track, dozens of kicks across the pattern. Build entire fills, ghost notes, accent layers, and humanized variations on a single track. No other drum machine plugin lets you go this deep without leaving the row.

### 10 LFOs + Shape Editor

Draw **custom LFO curves** with tension control. Sync to host, free-run, or trigger per step. Per-target bypass without losing depth. **5-stage AHDSR** envelopes with adjustable tension on every stage.

### Per-Step Parameter Locks

Every knob is automatable per-step. **Pitch, filter, distortion, synth shape, bit depth, sample rate** — anything. Shift-select steps, move a knob, done. Hit chance, sub-steps, and polyrhythm endpoints built in.

New

### Generative Fills (GEN)

**Euclidean fill** with a live HITS knob — hear the pattern morph as you turn. **Dice & density** seeded random fills, plus rotation arrows to shift any pattern left or right. Happy accidents, on demand.

New

### 808-Style Accents

A dedicated **per-step accent row** with a global accent amount — the classic hardware trick for grooves that breathe. Accents carry through to **MIDI export** so your DAW clip keeps the dynamics.

New

### Per-Track Speed

Run any track at **1/4× to 4× the pattern speed**, triplet ratios included. Half-time kicks under double-time hats, polytempo textures, instant drum & bass — all inside one pattern.

New

### Link Groups

Tag **steps or REX slices into colored groups** and edit them as one. Shift-click recalls a group's settings everywhere it appears. Build variations that stay consistent across the whole pattern.

New

### Multi-Out Slice Routing

Route **individual REX slices or whole link groups to separate plugin outputs**. Process the snare slices through your favorite bus chain while the rest stays on the main out. Remixing, solved.

### Vintage Lo-Fi Topology

Hardware-accurate sampler emulation. **Anti-aliasing, zero-order hold, noise-shaped bitcrushing**, and 3 character shapes: Arcade, Vintage Sampler, and Crunch. Per-track lo-fi without compromising the rest of the mix.

### BeatForge Sculptor EQ

Dedicated drum-tuned EQ with five musical bands: **Sub, Thump, Clean, Crack, and Air**. Each band targets specific drum frequencies — boost punch at 120Hz, snap at 3kHz, presence at 10kHz. Tube exciter on the high band.

### Full MIDI Control

MIDI Learn on every knob, every step, every button. Native **Launch Control XL** integration with bidirectional LED feedback. Full MIDI I/O per track. Drag-and-drop MIDI export of any pattern or REX slice.

### Smart Sample Engine

**AI-powered BPM detection** via Beat This!. Native REX support plus a built-in slicer for WAV/AIFF/FLAC/MP3/OGG. **Random sample & slice modes**, time-stretch or resample pitch, full undo/redo. Drag-and-drop everything.

## BeatForge In Action

More demos and walkthroughs on our [YouTube channel](https://www.youtube.com/@beatforgesequencer).

## User Manual

Master the workflow: from AI-assisted importing to per-step parameter locking.

BeatForge runs as a Standalone App and as a VST3/AU Plugin. The installer handles placement automatically.

#### Installation

*   **Windows:** Run the `.msi` installer. You can select which components to install (Standalone/VST3). The installer will automatically place plugins in the correct system folder (`C:\Program Files\Common Files\VST3`) so your DAW can find them.
*   **macOS:** Open the `.dmg` file. Drag the BeatForge app to your **Applications** folder. To install the plugins (VST3/AU), run the separate `.pkg` installer found inside the disk image.

#### Activation

*   **Free Trial:** On first launch, click "Start Trial" to begin a fully functional 14-day evaluation.
*   **Purchase:** Click the "Buy Now" button on our website. You will receive a license code via email from **Lemon Squeezy** immediately after purchase.
*   **Unlock:** Copy the code from your email and paste it into the BeatForge activation screen to unlock your lifetime license.

Navigate using the large buttons at the top of the interface:

*   **Sequencer:** The main hub. Manage tracks, sequencing, LFOs, and sound design (Mix, ADSR, FX, EQ, Filter, Synth).
*   **Mixer:** A dedicated view for quick **audio and MIDI routing**. Use this to assign Audio Outputs and set MIDI Input/Output channels for all tracks side-by-side. The Volume and Pan sliders here are **Master Controls** that sit _after_ the sequencer's internal mixing chain.
*   **Slicer:** The advanced sample editor. View waveforms, detect transients, and slice standard audio loops. _(Note: REX files are **read-only** in this view)._

BeatForge integrates the advanced [Beat This!](https://github.com/CPJKU/beat_this) AI algorithm to analyze samples instantly upon import.

*   **Drag & Drop:** Drop any WAV, AIFF, FLAC, MP3, OGG, or REX (.rx2) file onto a track slot.
*   **Auto-Sync:** The engine detects the sample's BPM automatically.
    *   If you play the loop **unsliced**, BeatForge automatically applies **Time Stretching** to lock it to your project tempo.
    *   If you **Slice** the loop, synchronization is handled by the sequencer triggering individual slices at the correct time, keeping your transients crisp without stretching artifacts.
*   **Manual Correction:** In the rare case the detected BPM is incorrect, you can click to enter it manually. You can also hold **Shift** and drag the grid lines near the end of the loop to fine-tune the BPM for a perfect match.

Create evolving patterns using Edit Groups, P-Locks, and probability.

#### Basic Sequencing

*   **Step Buttons:** Click to toggle steps. Right-Click a step to set **Hit Chance** (Probability), **Flam/Rolls**, or assign an Edit Group.
*   **Grid Size:** Right-click the **Track Name** to toggle the track between **16 Steps** (Standard) and **12 Steps** (Triplets/8T).
*   **Polymeters:** Alt + Click a step to set it as the "Bar Endpoint." That track will loop independently at that step count.
*   **Track Speed:** Set any track to run from **1/4× to 4×** the pattern speed, including triplet ratios — half-time and double-time feels inside one pattern.
*   **Accents:** Toggle the accent row to push individual steps harder, 808-style. The global **Accent** slider sets how much louder accented steps hit, and accents are preserved in MIDI export.

#### Edit Groups (32 per track)

Each track has **32 Edit Groups** — 32 fully independent sound configurations that can be assigned per step. Every knob position, synth type, distortion mode, EQ setting, and macro is stored separately per group. Use them for true per-step sound design within a single track:

*   **Switch Active Group:** Use the Group selector at the top of the Sequencer to choose which group your knob edits affect. The synth tab, FX, EQ — everything reflects the currently selected group.
*   **Assign Steps to Groups:** Scroll on a step button to assign it to a different group. The step shows a small group number when it's not Group 1.
*   **Use Cases:**
    *   Tight short kick on the downbeats, longer distorted kick on the off-beats — all on one track.
    *   Build a whole drum fill on one track by varying the synth type per group.
    *   Create ghost notes with different velocity, decay, and filter — without touching another track.
    *   Layer accent variations: open hat on beat 2 and 4, closed hat everywhere else, same track.

#### Parameter Locks (P-Locks)

Automate any knob per-step without complex menus:

*   **Select Step(s):** Hold **Shift** and click a step button (or multiple) to select them. They will remain highlighted.
*   **Lock Parameter:** With the step(s) selected, move _any_ slider (Pitch, Filter, Decay, etc.). The slider will indicate a lock is set for those steps.
*   **Clear Lock:** Right-Click any step to turn off its locks. To clear multiple steps at once, **Shift-Select** them first, then **Right-Click**.

The **GEN tab** writes patterns for you — as a starting point or a finished groove.

*   **Euclidean Fill:** Turn the **HITS** knob to spread that many hits as evenly as possible across the track. You hear the pattern update live while turning; release to commit, or leave the knob where it started to keep your original pattern.
*   **Dice & Density:** **DICE** rolls a new random pattern, **DENS** sets how busy it gets. The same dice value always produces the same pattern at a given density, so you can dial density up and down without losing a roll you like.
*   **Rotate:** The arrow buttons shift the whole pattern left or right by one step. Modifier keys change what gets rotated — try them with Shift and Alt held.

Everything the GEN tab writes is a normal pattern afterwards — edit steps, add parameter locks, assign edit groups as usual. And if you don't like a result: full **undo/redo** has your back.

BeatForge ships with 30+ circuit-modeled drum synths. Switch any track to "Synth" mode and pick from the dropdown.

#### 808 Kit (Eight-O)

Full 808 model: Kick, Snare, Closed Hat, Open Hat, Cymbal, Cowbell, Clap, Low/Mid/Hi Tom, Low/Mid/Hi Conga, Rimshot, Claves, Maracas.

#### 909 Kit (Nine-O)

Full 909 model: Kick, Snare, Rimshot, Clap, Low/Mid/Hi Tom, Closed Hat, Open Hat, Crash, Ride.

#### Bass & Lead Synths

*   **Vintage DCO:** Classic analog-style oscillator. Warm, characterful, great for chiptune and 80s tones.
*   **Hoover:** The iconic gabber/hardcore lead. Detuned, raw, ready to mangle.
*   **Reese Bass:** Thick, detuned bass with wobble character. Drum & bass essential.
*   **Supersaw:** Seven detuned saws stacked. Trance/EDM/euphoric leads.
*   **FM Kick:** Frequency modulation kick for punchy, modern electronic kicks beyond what 808/909 can do.
*   **Karplus Snare:** Physical modeling with coupled drum skins (A/B interaction) and snare wires for realistic acoustic response.

#### Macro Knobs

Every synth has 4 macro controls: **Shape, Timbre, Color, Aux**. The exact parameters they control vary by synth, but the macros are always musically useful — twist them to discover the engine's range.

BeatForge features two distinct slicing engines depending on your file type.

#### 1\. REX Files (.rx2)

REX files are loaded **Read-Only**. There is no need to open the Slicer tab for these, as the slice markers are pre-defined (use Reason Studios' ReCycle to edit them). Instead, focus on the **Playback Mode (S/L/C)** toggle next to the track name:

*   **Slice (S):** Play individual slices using MIDI notes. (_Tip: Ensure your track's **MIDI Input Channel** is set correctly in the **Mixer Tab** to receive notes from your DAW_).
*   **Loop (L):** The loop plays continuously, locked to the host tempo.
*   **Sequence (C):** "Creative" mode. Allows you to select a specific start slice/sequence and trigger it using BeatForge's internal step buttons.

**MIDI Export:** You can drag the REX MIDI pattern directly from the track to your DAW timeline.

#### Working With Slices

*   **Mute a Slice:** Option/Alt + Click a slice to mute it in place — it stays muted until you unmute it or load a new file. Great for thinning out busy loops.
*   **Slice Parameter Locks:** Select a slice and move any knob to lock that setting to the slice. Slice locks apply _both_ in the internal sequencer and when triggering via MIDI from your DAW.
*   **Link Groups:** Right-click a slice to tag it into a colored group. Shift + Click a group chip to recall its settings on every slice in the group.
*   **Per-Slice Outputs:** Right-click a slice (or a link group) to override its audio output. Anything without an override falls back to the track's Main output.
*   **Random Slice Mode:** Enable Random on the track and every trigger picks a fresh slice — muted and empty slices are skipped automatically.

#### 2\. Audio Slicer (WAV/AIFF/FLAC)

For standard audio, open the **Slicer Tab** to access the full editor.

*   **Editing:** Use the **Sensitivity** slider to auto-detect transients, or manually Move, Delete, and **Lock** slice markers directly on the waveform.
*   **MIDI Export:** Once you've sliced the loop, drag the MIDI pattern directly from the track to your DAW timeline to generate a matching MIDI clip — one note per slice.

High-quality time-stretching and pitch-shifting are powered by [Signalsmith Stretch](https://signalsmith-audio.co.uk/code/stretch/) by Geraint Luff / Signalsmith Audio, used under the MIT License.

#### Distortion (5 modes)

Right-click the Distortion knob to select one of 5 algorithms:

*   **Hard:** Aggressive digital clipping.
*   **Foldback:** Wave-folding distortion that adds metallic, resonant harmonics.
*   **Hardcore:** Extreme high-gain drive (10x boost) with a soft-knee limiter.
*   **Tube:** Authentic **Koren 12AX7 valve modeling**. Asymmetric saturation with real second-harmonic content. The most musical of the five — use for analog warmth and natural character.

#### Vintage Lo-Fi (Bitcrusher + Decimator)

Two sliders give you authentic hardware-sampler character:

*   **Bit Depth:** Reduce from 16-bit down to 2-bit. Right-click to select shape:
    *   _Arcade:_ Clean digital quantization.
    *   _Vintage Sampler:_ Aggressive noise-shaped quantization with vintage character.
    *   _Crunch:_ Hybrid — between clean and vintage.
*   **Sample Rate:** Hardware-accurate downsampling with proper anti-aliasing, zero-order hold, and DAC-style reconstruction filtering. Get authentic SP-1200 or MPC60 character without digital nastiness.

Five drum-tuned bands targeting specific musical frequencies:

*   **Sub (60Hz boost + 100Hz cut):** Classic Pultec sub trick. Adds weight without mud.
*   **Thump (120Hz):** Chest-hitting punch.
*   **Clean (400Hz):** Boxy lower-mid control.

*   **Tension Curves:** Adjust the slope of the Attack, Decay, and Release stages independently. Morph from **Logarithmic** (snappy) to **Exponential** (lazy).

*   **Presets:** Sine, Square, Saw Up, Saw Down — all built from the same node system, so you can modify them after loading.
*   **Sync Modes:** Tempo-synced (1/16th, 1/8, 1/4, etc. with triplet/dotted options) or free-running in Hz.
*   **Trigger Modes:** Global (always running), Trigger (restart on each note), Envelope (one-shot on trigger).

*   **Bipolar/Unipolar:** Right-click to toggle modulation polarity.
*   **Bypass:** Right-click "Bypass LFO" to temporarily disable without losing your depth setting. Perfect for live A/B comparison.
*   **Live Visualization:** The modulated knob shows the LFO's current value as a moving dot on the ring.

*   **Mute/Solo Buttons:** Assign to hardware buttons for tactile mixing.
*   **Step Buttons:** Assign to pads or step sequencer hardware for direct programming.

text:
# about.html

### A bit of history.

30 odd years ago. Programmers saw that basing music on small, pitch shifted, samples could save a lot of resources. So "Tracker" players were created and developed. These were based on a trimmed version of how MIDI worked.

Today, there is no longer a need to trim the MIDI standard, and so we can keep the advantages of interoperability together with custom quality of pitch shifted samples. Also we can blend samples from different frequencies and then envelope the results for even better results.

MIDI files on computers suffered from some bad sounding implementation in the past and as such lost some popularity in some sectors. However, in the music profession it is the defacto standard for good implementations.

### Why.

Instead of transferring huge audio tracks, we can just send short samples (or none at all and calculate them) and play them at extremely low computational cost. Realistically you can reduce the footprint of your audio media sizes by factors of 100 or more! Obviously this is not a fit for all cases, but you may be surprised how far it can fit. It also offers the ability to nicely bridge between tracks which can be a useful effect.

### Timeline.

**1990's:**

SongForge started life as a tracker in the 90's, It was a utility for playing music in games.

**2014:**

Awoke from deep sleep, It took the form of a mobile app and became more widely used. It was also used as a Unity engine for a few games.

**2024:**

The project was awoke again it is transformed into into something far more musical. It became super-charged into quite an able DAW and supports the web natively. I use it to get kids interested and creative.

It is now actively developed and expanded.

### Who.

The project has always been lead and developed by Aaron Booth, who has been developing for decades.

## Query: "site:hitlab.com HITLAB music technology Digital Emerging Artist Competition"

On Hitlab’s site, the relevant term appears to be **DEAS = Digital Emerging Artist Showcase**, not “Digital Emerging Artist Competition.” It’s described as a music-tech talent discovery platform powered by Hitlab’s AI/DNA song analysis tools.

Key findings:
- Hitlab says DEAS is the “world’s first fully digital global emerging artist showcase” for independent artists to submit songs, get scored for hit potential, and potentially receive label/publishing opportunities ([Our Products](https://hitlab.com/our-products/)).
- Hitlab’s homepage positions the company as a music-tech/AI platform for discovering “tomorrow’s hitmakers” ([Home](https://hitlab.com/)).
- A Hitlab process memo says its proprietary tools include **DNA** and **DEAS (Digital Emerging Artist Showcase)** to discover independent music globally ([Process Memo PDF](https://hitlab.com/wp-content/uploads/2024/08/The-HITLAB-Process-Memo.pdf)).
- A Hitlab FAQ references a separate **“Hitlab x Grammy Emerging Artists Competition”**, indicating “competition” language exists on the site, but in a different context from DEAS ([FAQ PDF](https://hitlab.com/wp-content/uploads/2023/02/QA.pdf)).
- A 2024 press release says Hitlab’s technology evaluates submissions from emerging artists worldwide and may lead to recording contracts ([Press Release PDF](https://hitlab.com/wp-content/uploads/2024/05/Hitlab-Cheese-press-release-April-23-2024.pdf)).

If you want, I can next extract the exact DEAS workflow or find the oldest/first mention of it on hitlab.com.

# Research: Preliminary naming clearance for rhythm-game and beatmap-editor candidates

## Summary

None of the eight candidates looks clean enough to adopt without changing course. **BeatForge, SongForge, BeatLab, SongLab, and JamForge are the most direct collisions**, because exact or effectively exact names are already used for music creation, rhythm, or kid-oriented music software. **TuneLab, HitLab, and PlayLab also carry high risk** through established adjacent music/software uses, a child-entertainment filing, or crowded exact-name use.

This is a preliminary public-web screen, not a comprehensive trademark search and not legal advice. A trademark lawyer should confirm live status, ownership, priority, related goods, state and international records, and common-law rights before launch.

## Findings

### Ranked collision screen

| Rank | Candidate | Preliminary risk | Concrete findings | Recommendation |
|---:|---|---|---|---|
| 1 | **SongForge** | **Critical** | An exact-name project says SongForge began as game music software, became a mobile app and Unity game engine, and is now an actively developed web-native DAW used to get kids creative. That overlaps the proposed product unusually closely. The active product is hosted at `songforge.makegl.com`; the separately surfaced `songforge.org` did not resolve during fetch, so domain ownership/status still needs registrar checking. [SongForge history](https://songforge.makegl.com/about.html) | Reject for this product absent extraordinary contrary legal findings. |
| 2 | **BeatForge** | **Critical** | `beatforge.nl` markets BeatForge as a paid standalone app plus VST3/AU drum machine and step sequencer, with pattern editing, sample slicing, MIDI export, and AI BPM detection. An Apple listing for “Beat Forge” also surfaced as a rhythm/music game, although that listing returned a not-found page on follow-up and should be rechecked by territory. The product domain is active, and `beatforge.net` also surfaced in search. [BeatForge product](https://beatforge.nl/) [Apple listing surfaced in search](https://apps.apple.com/us/app/beat-forge-int/id6759181420) | Reject. It collides in both music-production software and the rhythm-game channel. |
| 3 | **BeatLab** | **Critical** | `beatlab.app` is an active suite of iOS/Android rhythm-training apps for dancers and musicians, featuring beat counting, instrument isolation, and tempo adjustment. Apple also has an exact “BeatLab - Metronome & Beats” app. A BEAT LAB design registration was surfaced at U.S. Reg. No. 5,599,403, and BEATLAB ACADEMY serial 87800207 covered music-production education but is shown abandoned. Existing app and common-law use are material even if a particular filing is dead or design-only. [BeatLab product](https://beatlab.app/en/) [Apple app](https://apps.apple.com/us/app/beatlab-metronome-beats/id6755929889) [BEAT LAB index](https://uspto.report/TM/87800205/) [BEATLAB ACADEMY record](https://trademarks.justia.com/878/00/beat-lab-87800207.html) | Reject. Exact active music/rhythm app use is too close. |
| 4 | **SongLab** | **Critical** | Two exact-name music apps surfaced in Apple’s store: “SongLab: Lyrics & Takes” and “AI Song & Music Maker-SongLab.” A U.S. standard-character SONGLAB application, serial 98804771, claims SaaS using AI to generate, sort, transcribe, and mix music, with first use claimed October 14, 2024. Its index status was “new application” and must be refreshed in official TSDR. [Lyrics & Takes](https://apps.apple.com/us/app/songlab-lyrics-takes/id6757769537) [AI music maker](https://apps.apple.com/us/app/ai-song-music-maker-songlab/id6504249228) [SONGLAB record](https://trademarks.justia.com/988/04/songlab-98804771.html) | Reject. Multiple exact music-app uses plus a highly relevant federal application. |
| 5 | **JamForge** | **High to critical** | `jamforge.org` surfaced as an exact-name music web app offering AI bandmates and generated songs/jam tracks. The site could not be fetched directly during verification, so recency and operator details remain uncertain, but the exact music-service result is a serious common-law/domain warning. A separate exact JamForge Unity framework is distributed through OpenUPM, increasing software-channel crowding. [JamForge](https://jamforge.org/) [OpenUPM JamForge](https://openupm.com/packages/com.anomyx.jamforge/) | Do not advance unless counsel first verifies whether the music service is active and its geographic priority. Even if inactive, it is not a clean candidate. |
| 6 | **TuneLab** | **High** | TuneLab is longstanding professional piano-tuning software for Windows, iOS, and Android. Apple lists “TuneLab Piano Tuner,” and the official `tunelab-world.com` site offers current platform versions and downloads. No clearly relevant exact federal record surfaced in this screen, but exact commercial use for downloadable music software is enough to create substantial concern. [Official Windows product](https://www.tunelab-world.com/tlp.html) [Official iOS product](https://www.tunelab-world.com/tl-iphone.html) [Apple app](https://apps.apple.com/us/app/tunelab-piano-tuner/id335568329) | Reject for a music app. The goods and app-store channel are close despite different functions. |
| 7 | **HitLab** | **High** | `hitlab.com` is an established music-technology brand using AI/DNA song analysis and a Digital Emerging Artist Showcase to evaluate music and discover artists. An older music-related HITLAB application surfaced as abandoned, while a live-looking exact HITLAB registration surfaced for unrelated healthcare innovation. App-store exact hits were mostly sports/fitness, but the music company and exact domain are the important collision. [HITLAB](https://hitlab.com/) [HITLAB products](https://hitlab.com/our-products/) [Older music filing index](https://www.trademarkelite.com/trademark/trademark-detail/77659504/HITLAB) [Healthcare HITLAB record](https://trademarks.justia.com/869/81/hitlab-86981119.html) | Reject. Exact, established music-tech brand use outweighs the absence of a verified live music-software federal record in this screen. |
| 8 | **PlayLab** | **High** | PLAYLAB is crowded across software, education, and children’s entertainment. `playlab.ai` is an active AI software platform for education. Apple has “Playlab Classroom,” an educational children’s app with mini-games. U.S. serial 98678289 is an exact standard-character PLAYLAB application for children’s interactive play, music and dance classes, claiming use since 2017. This is less directly musical than the top collisions, but the proposed kid-friendly game sits close to its audience and entertainment context. [Playlab AI](https://www.playlab.ai/) [Playlab Classroom](https://apps.apple.com/us/app/playlab-classroom/id1540914694) [PLAYLAB record](https://trademarks.justia.com/986/78/playlab-98678289.html) | Weakest of a bad set, but still not recommended. Only candidate arguably worth lawyer triage if business strongly prefers it, not because it appears clear. |

### Cross-cutting conclusions

1. **The “music word + Lab/Forge” field is crowded.** Every candidate produced exact public use, and seven produced exact music, software, game, or child-entertainment use. The suffix variation does not create a meaningful clean lane on this evidence.
2. **Same-market collision is strongest for SongForge and BeatForge.** SongForge describes essentially a kid-oriented DAW/game-music tool, while BeatForge is active beat-production and sequencing software. These are more probative than merely unrelated trademark hits. [SongForge](https://songforge.makegl.com/about.html) [BeatForge](https://beatforge.nl/)
3. **App-store uniqueness is already lost for several names.** BeatLab, SongLab, and TuneLab have exact Apple music-app listings. PlayLab has a child education app, and Beat Forge surfaced as a rhythm app, subject to territory/status verification.
4. **Domain practicality is poor.** Exact branded domains are already visibly in use for `beatlab.app`, `hitlab.com`, `jamforge.org`, `beatforge.nl`, `tunelab-world.com`, and `playlab.ai`; SongForge has an active branded subdomain. This screen did not run registrar/RDAP checks for the bare `.com` of every candidate, so availability, ownership, and aftermarket price remain unverified.
5. **Trademark-index results are leads, not final status opinions.** Third-party indexes can lag. The exact SONGLAB and PLAYLAB applications should be refreshed in [USPTO Trademark Search](https://www.uspto.gov/trademarks/search/search) and [TSDR](https://tsdr.uspto.gov/), including Class 9 game/downloadable software, Class 41 entertainment, and Class 42 SaaS, plus spacing and phonetic variants.

### Candidate worth deeper legal clearance

**No candidate earns a positive clearance recommendation from this set.** If a stakeholder insists on preserving one, **PlayLab** is the least directly occupied in rhythm-game or beatmap-editor software, but it still has an exact child entertainment/music application and active education software/app uses. A narrow lawyer-led knockout search could determine whether those rights are fatal, but a fresh, more distinctive name is likely more efficient.

### Dropped or down-weighted

- Generic name-generator, SEO, social-media, artist-name, and unrelated product results - Too weak to assess source confusion for the proposed goods.
- Healthcare HITLAB - Retained only as crowding context, not treated as a same-market blocker.
- BEATLAB ACADEMY abandoned filing and older abandoned music HITLAB filing - Not treated as live federal rights, only evidence of naming history and possible common-law use.
- `songforge.org` - Search surfaced it, but DNS resolution failed during direct fetch, so it was not relied on as current product evidence.
- Apple “Beat Forge” rhythm listing - Search surfaced it, but the page returned not found on follow-up, so it was treated as a verification lead rather than decisive evidence.

## Gaps and residual risks

- No attorney-supervised comprehensive search was performed across USPTO live/dead records, state registries, WIPO/Madrid, EUIPO, UKIPO, Canada, or other launch markets.
- Exact status and prosecution history for indexed filings must be checked in TSDR by serial number. Similarity searches should include spaces, plurals, phonetics, design marks, owners, and related goods.
- Common-law priority, actual geographic reach, sales, reputation, coexistence agreements, and litigation/opposition history were not established.
- Bare-domain availability was not verified by RDAP/registrar for every TLD, and a domain being available would not establish trademark clearance.
- App-store availability varies by country and time. Google Play coverage was less complete than Apple coverage.
- A distinctive new coined mark should undergo the same legal clearance before investment.
